general_guidelines: Ensure cooling equipment (fans, air conditioning) is operational.
Check that volunteer contacts and database are up to date.

Yellow.trigger: Heatwave temperatures likely to impact vulnerable groups.
general_guidelines: Initiate regular check-ins with registered vulnerable residents.
Remind community of hydration best practices.
Publicize cooling hub locations and opening hours.

Amber.trigger: Heatwave temperatures likely to affect wider population and infrastructure.
general_guidelines: Launch active door-knocking volunteer campaigns in high-risk zones.
Distribute cool water and advice pamphlets to vulnerable homes.
Coordinate transport support to cooling hubs for high-risk individuals.

Red.trigger: Severe heatwave threatening lives of healthy individuals as well.
general_guidelines: Mobilize emergency volunteer units for 24/7 resident monitoring.
Establish direct communication channels with local health services.
Provide emergency cooling support and immediate relocation assistance.

elderly.clinical_risks: Reduced sweating capability, higher risk of dehydration, cardiovascular strain.
actions: Check that indoor temperature is under 26°C.
Verify they have at least 2 liters of drinking water within easy reach.
Check medication labels (some medicines impair heat tolerance or require refrigeration).
Advise keeping curtains closed on sun-facing windows.

infants.clinical_risks: Rapid dehydration, inability to regulate body temperature, risk of heat stroke.
actions: Advise parents to keep rooms cool (ideally 16-20°C, definitely under 24°C).
Ensure babies are dressed in light, breathable clothing (e.g., single singlet/nappy).
Provide frequent breastfeeds or water/formula feeds.
Never leave children in parked cars under any circumstances.

chronic_illness.vulnerability_type: Chronic Illness (Heart, Lung, or Kidney conditions)
chronic_illness.clinical_risks: Heat increases blood viscosity and workload on the heart/lungs, potentially exacerbating underlying diseases.
actions: Ensure inhalers, heart medication, or other critical treatments are easily accessible.
Advise staying indoors in air-conditioned or well-ventilated cool rooms.
Record and monitor pulse rate and blood pressure if they have monitoring devices.
